author: snowkot
text: Liverpool 2026/27 Season - Iraola Officially Confirmed18 minutes agoReplyQuoteSelect PostDeselect PostLink to PostMemberGive GiftBack to TopPost by snowkot on18 minutes agoConsidering the way Iraola's sets his teams up, versus Alonso, who made Wirtz and Frimpong, against our current squad, incoming transfers excluded. Ran this through a Claude AI session over some time. Not stimulus - response. Prompts back and forth, as all AI engagement must be, to solve for our best possible set up. Hence me with AI, writing is mine.Three shapes, not two: 4-4-2 without the ball, 4-2-3-1 on the teamsheet, 3-2-5 with it. The teamsheet shape is the one that exists for about four seconds at kickoff and in the matchday graphic — the other two are where the team actually lives.Out of possession:In the 4-4-2, the nominal left winger becomes the left midfielder — tucking in, tracking the opposition right-back, doing a full shift in the block. That's the duty that ground Wirtz down under Slot. So the complete solution isn't just "give him the half-space pocket in possession" — it's managing his defensive assignment so the pocket arrives with legs left to use it.Iraola has 3 options: accept the trade on big days (Wirtz presses high in the first wave instead, swapping duties with Szoboszlai situationally), hide him on the side of the opposition's weaker fullback, or — the Klopp solution with Salah, ironically — let the left side's work-rate be subsidized by Kerkez and the left-sided 8 covering behind him.Hence nominal 4-2-3-1 teamsheet shape, but Iraola's actual out-of-possession structure collapses into a 4-4-2, which is the signature of his entire defensive scheme.In possession:An asymmetric 3-2-5, and it solves the squad's three biggest problems in one rotation. Gomez tucks in from right-back to form a back three, giving a permanent 3-2 base behind the ball — so a 35-year-old Van Dijk and two teenagers coming off surgery are never left defending a naked high line when the press gets beaten. That tuck releases Frimpong from all build-up and defensive responsibility into the pure right-wingback role he played at Leverkusen — the only position he's ever been elite in, and the one neither Liverpool manager has yet let him occupy. On the other side, Wirtz abandons the "LW" on the teamsheet and lives in the left half-space pocket, receiving between the lines on the half-turn exactly as Alonso staged it during the invincible season, with Kerkez supplying the touchline width so he never has to hug chalk and Szoboszlai taking the nominal 10 slot to do the box-crashing and running. Isak pins the centre-backs as the reference point. Five lanes occupied, the £116M playmaker on his actual throne, the wingback restored, and cover behind it all — the standard modern possession rotation, mapped onto exactly the personnel this squad was built from.Out of possession: 4-2-3-1 → 4-4-2AlissonKerkez – VVD – Leoni – GomezGravenberch – Mac AllisterWirtz - Szoboszlai - FrimpongIsakIn possession → 3-2-5:VVD  –  Leoni  –  Gomez)Kerkez takes the left touchline / Frimpong pushes to pure right wingback (his Leverkusen role back)Wirtz drifts off the LW slot into the left half-space pocketSzoboszlai does the pressing-10 runningIsak pins the centre.
